skillName: Write integers in words and figures
misconceptionSummary: Students write 'and' between every group or use it incorrectly (e.g. 'four thousand and two hundred and six').

steps.narration: We need to write this number in words. The zero is the tricky part — it holds a position but we don't say it.

lines.text: Right of comma: 206 → 'two hundred and six'

steps.narration: The comma is your dividing line. Everything to the left gets 'thousand' attached. Everything to the right is written as a number under a thousand.

visuals.ruleText: 'And' goes before the final part — only if that part is less than 100
visuals.subText: four thousand two hundred AND six ✓   (not: four thousand AND two hundred AND six)

steps.narration: There's only one 'and' in the whole number. It sits right before the last section — but only when that section is less than a hundred. Put it anywhere else and it's wrong.

visuals.expression: four thousand two hundred and six
visuals.label: 4,206 written in words

steps.narration: One 'and', right before the six. The zero tens are simply silent.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: 'four thousand AND two hundred AND six'. Only one 'and' — before the final part when it is under 100.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If you hear yourself saying 'and' more than once, stop. There should only ever be one 'and' in the whole number.
